meta.title: Unit 1 — Singular & Plural Nouns (Test 4)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Apply plural noun rules to animals and everyday objects.

questions.question: What is the plural of "panda"?
questions.explanation: panda → pandas. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: "Owls" is the plural form of "owl".
questions.explanation: Yes! owl → owls. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Write the plural of "snake".
questions.explanation: snake → snakes. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: The ___ eats the bugs. (frog — only one)
questions.explanation: "One" tells us we need a singular noun: frog.

questions.question: The plural of "octopus" is "octopuses".
questions.explanation: Yes! octopus ends in -s, so we add -es: octopus → octopuses.

questions.question: Write the plural of "fox".
questions.explanation: fox → foxes. Words ending in -x add -es.

questions.question: I plant two ___ in the yard. (bush)
questions.explanation: bush → bushes. Words ending in -sh add -es.

questions.question: Sort these animal nouns: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: panda/snake = one animal (singular). foxes/owls/octopuses = more than one (plural).

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "Some bears eat fish."

questions.question: The plural of "road" is "roads".
questions.explanation: Yes! road → roads. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Write the plural of "dish".
questions.explanation: dish → dishes. Words ending in -sh add -es.

questions.question: Which plural follows the -es rule?
questions.explanation: bus ends in -s, so we add -es: bus → buses.

questions.question: Sort these plurals: did they add -s or -es?
questions.explanation: Regular nouns add -s. Nouns ending in -x or -sh add -es.

questions.question: In the sentence "There are many stars in the sky," the noun "stars" is singular.
questions.explanation: "many stars" shows there are more than one — "stars" is plural.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "The frog jumps on the lily pad."

questions.question: Write the plural of "ostrich".
questions.explanation: ostrich → ostriches. Words ending in -ch add -es.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: "five" tells us we need plural. car → cars.

questions.question: What is the plural of "cockroach"?
questions.explanation: cockroach ends in -ch, so we add -es: cockroach → cockroaches.

questions.question: In the sentence "The panda eats bamboo," the noun "panda" is plural.
questions.explanation: "The panda" (with "the" and no number) means just one panda — singular.

questions.question: Write the plural of "bus".
questions.explanation: bus → buses. Words ending in -s add -es.
